name: Minimum Initial Energy to Finish Tasks
title: Minimum Initial Energy to Finish Tasks

discussion_posts.name: kaint
discussion_posts.content: I don\'t know why people are posting solution if they are not explaining,\nDuring contest I was thiking to use DP, binary search on value, sorting, but didn\'t reach upto sorting by  difference. \nCan anyone please tell me, how they reach upto that, it would be helpful.

discussion_posts.name: gautamsharma22
discussion_posts.content: Saw the same in OA of Goldman Sacs :)

discussion_posts.name: iAditya1441
discussion_posts.content: Sorting with difference in actual and minimum is not at all intutive in nature

discussion_posts.name: Java_Programmer_Ujjwal
discussion_posts.content: One more greedy problem where i failed :(

discussion_posts.name: panzerschrek
discussion_posts.content: For some non-obvious reason sorting by difference between `minimum` and `actual` works. What kind of black magic is this?

discussion_posts.name: panzerschrek
discussion_posts.content: I tried to sort tasks by `minimum` - it seems to be reasonable. But somehow this doesn\'t work for example 2. WTF? How one suppose to solve it, if such obvious heuristic doesn\'t work here?

discussion_posts.name: freakyshadows
discussion_posts.content:
For the folks wondering about sorting idea
The trick is in the constraints  `actual​ (i)<= minimum(i)`
meaning the we need more energy to start less to finish
so after every task we will have `minimum- actual` as saved energy
let's say `[ [1,100],[2,5],[3,5] ]
` if it is so high the saved energy from first task can be roll out on last two task

discussion_posts.name: Consistent_
discussion_posts.content: Everyone who isn\'t able to do it , Wake up to reality and ask yourself what makes you so weak to not be able to apply binary search. Stop giving excuses to yourself that question is hard, because hardly anyone cares. Do the struggle on your own.

discussion_posts.name: Zaid_Shaikh001
discussion_posts.content:
sort by (minimum - actual) because a greater (minimum - actual)
value requires a high starting energy but does not consume much
actual energy hence we might have enough energy to start other tasks as well.

discussion_posts.name: anandravishankar_sbu
discussion_posts.content: For example 3, isn\'t 26 the right answer? I did a top down approach with the following transition function dp[i] = dp[i-1] + abs(tasks[i][1] - (tasks[i-1][1] - tasks[i-1][0])). 26 satisfies all the tasks when done in decreasing order.
